{{Quote|Logray must guide us in matters such as this.|Arbo|<ref name="AOT" />}}
'''Arbo''' was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] who lived in [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] as the village's [[legend keeper]]. When it was revealed that the [[Wokling/Legends|wokling]] [[Malani]] had been kidnapped, Arbo suggested that the Ewoks ask their [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], for advice before any decisions were made.

==Biography==
{{Quote|He is the oldest and wisest and is close to the ways of the Spirit.|Arbo|<ref name="AOT" />}}
[[File:Logray3.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Arbo was an admirer of Logray (pictured).]]
Arbo was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] member of the [[Bright Tree Village tribe]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. The [[legend keeper]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]],<ref name="AOT" /> Arbo was a sage member of the tribe.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|39|Arbo}}</ref> At one point, a band of [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] entered [[Happy Grove/Legends|Happy Grove]], the area around Arbo's [[City/Legends|village]]; the visitors, led by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Vulgarr/Legends|Vulgarr]], claimed that a [[Grudakk|giant creature]] had kidnapped [[Malani]], one of the village's [[Youngling/Legends|young]] [[Wokling/Legends|woklings]]. Vulgarr implored the Ewoks to send as many [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] as they could spare to fight the beast and reclaim the child, but Arbo urged caution and suggested that they consult the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], first.<ref name="AOT" />

Nevertheless, [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], the leader of Bright Tree's Ewoks, ordered the tribe's warriors to pursue the creature. Once the shaman had arrived, Arbo quickly told him the story. However, Chirpa's strategy to leave the [[Gender/Legends|women]] and elders to guard the village and its woklings left the village vulnerable, allowing Vulgarr and his band to take advantage of the lowered village defenses by kidnapping the remaining children. Arbo's name was shared by<ref name="AOT" /> the [[Arbo Maze]], a [[Cave/Legends|cave]] located on Endor.<ref name="tree">{{Ewoks|The Tree of Light}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We will send for Logray!|Arbo|<ref name="AOT" />}}
Arbo was a great admirer of Logray,<ref name="CSWE" /> urging that the Ewoks consult with him prior to making a decision.<ref name="AOT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arbo first appeared in ''[[The Adventures of Teebo: A Tale of Magic and Suspense]]'', a young-readers book written by [[Joe Johnston]] and published in [[1984]].<ref name="AOT" />

==Appearances==
*''[[The Adventures of Teebo: A Tale of Magic and Suspense]]'' {{1st}}
